heading: Civil damages for failure to release lien
label: 7432 Civil damages for failure to release lien
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 7432 was renumbered 7437 of this title.

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title VI, § 6240(c)102 Stat. 3747Nov. 10, 1988“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply to notices provided by the taxpayer of the failure to release a lien, and damages arising, after .”December 31, 1988
amendments: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title VI, § 6240(c)102 Stat. 3747Nov. 10, 1988“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply to notices provided by the taxpayer of the failure to release a lien, and damages arising, after .”December 31, 1988

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: If any officer or employee of the Internal Revenue Service knowingly, or by reason of negligence, fails to release a lien under section 6325 on property of the taxpayer, such taxpayer may bring a civil action for damages against the United States in a district court of the United States.
actual, direct economic damages sustained by the plaintiff which, but for the actions of the defendant, would not have been sustained, plus

children.heading: Payment authority
children.label: c Payment authority
children.text: Claims pursuant to this section shall be payable out of funds appropriated under , United States Code.section 1304 of title 31

children.heading: Requirement that administrative remedies be exhausted
children.label: 1 Requirement that administrative remedies be exhausted
children.text: A judgment for damages shall not be awarded under subsection (b) unless the court determines that the plaintiff has exhausted the administrative remedies available to such plaintiff within the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: Mitigation of damages
children.label: 2 Mitigation of damages
children.text: The amount of damages awarded under subsection (b)(1) shall be reduced by the amount of such damages which could have reasonably been mitigated by the plaintiff.

children.heading: Period for bringing action
children.label: 3 Period for bringing action
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, an action to enforce liability created under this section may be brought without regard to the amount in controversy and may be brought only within 2 years after the date the right of action accrues.

children.heading: Notice of failure to release lien
children.label: e Notice of failure to release lien
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulation prescribe reasonable procedures for a taxpayer to notify the Secretary of the failure to release a lien under section 6325 on property of the taxpayer.
